codeReview.summary: This fix improves documentation and adds a reusable verification script, but it still does not enforce finalization in the validator runtime path, and its default required-report set is not milestone-safe.

issues.description: Enforcement is still procedural ('Call enforcement script BEFORE synthesis') rather than wired into validator finalization logic. The commit only changes docs + a standalone script, and repository-wide search shows no runtime invocation path beyond documentation references, so validators can still report success without this check being executed.

Default required reports are hardcoded to `settings-ui.json launch-contracts.json`, which are core-engine-specific. Other milestones use different report names (e.g., fast-surfaces uses dashboard/popover), so default execution can fail valid runs or enforce the wrong artifact contract.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The assigned skill remains mismatched for this validator-infrastructure feature, and the transcript shows substantial discovery overhead before implementation.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: features.json assigns `skillName: switcher-integration-worker` to this feature; transcript skeleton for session `18d9d52b-1bac-45df-9d9d-0455a9856b67` shows repeated exploration of validator/droid locations outside switcher scope.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission shared-state docs do not identify where user-testing validator runtime behavior is actually defined, which increases time spent reverse-locating enforcement points.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Mission AGENTS.md is switcher-focused and `.factory/library/architecture.md` covers product architecture but not validator runtime ownership; transcript shows detours through `~/.factory/droids/user-testing-flow-validator.md` discovery.

summary: Review failed: compared to the prior failed feature, this rework adds a useful verification script and clearer guidance, but it still does not make validator success contingent on an enforced runtime finalization check, and the script’s default artifact contract is not generic across milestones.
